title: Moving to O'Fallon, MO
content:
O'Fallon is Missouri's most populated suburb (~95,000 residents), largest city in St. Charles County, ranked 7th statewide.

LOCATION: St. Charles County along I-64 and I-70. Downtown STL 45 min, Forest Park 30 min, St. Charles 10-15 min.

COST OF LIVING & SAFETY:
- Housing 15.1% below national average
- Energy 9.5% lower, transportation 11.7% less, healthcare 6.7% lower
- 62% feel pretty safe, 38% feel very safe, 64% report visible police presence

HOUSING:
- Median single-family: $347,500, condos $280,000
- Established neighborhoods: Northern O'Fallon ($200s-$300s), Parkview (upper $200s-$500s), Highway P and Main (low $200s-upper $300s)
- New developments: Streets of Caledonia ($329K-$906K), Sommerlin (from $413K), Harvest at Hopewell (~900 homes), Shady Creek (pools, lazy rivers), Amberleigh ($386K-$625K)

SCHOOLS:
- Fort Zumwalt: all 4 high schools in America's Best (U.S. News) 6 consecutive years, top 30 in MO
- Francis Howell: top-rated in St. Charles County, 13th statewide
- Wentzville R-IV: Liberty High School, Discovery Ridge Elementary

RECREATION:
- Fort Zumwalt Park (48 acres), Dames Park (59 acres), Ozzie Smith Sports Complex (76 acres)
- Alligator's Creek Aquatic Center, Renaud Center, YMCA
- 15 off-leash dog parks
- O'Fallon Hoots collegiate baseball

DINING & BREWERIES: Goodwood Brewing, Good News Brewing, Crossroads Brewery, Shortleaf Brewing

EVENTS: Heritage and Freedom Fest (July), Fall into the Arts (Sept), Celebration of Lights

RANKINGS: #21 Best Places to Live in Midwest (Consumer Affairs 2025), Fortune Best Places for Families (2024)
